description: Maps scenario IDs to their branch names and expected outcomes. Revert scenarios (4.x) map to the table in gh-audit's Architecture.md and TODO.md.

4.1.title: AutoRevert (bot-style message)
4.1.notes: Message matches `Automatic revert of <sha>..<sha>` regex; trusted by construction without diff verification.

4.3.title: Conflict-resolved git-revert
4.3.expected_verdict: non-compliant (falls through to PR-approval; no approval on this PR)

4.4.title: Manual revert with stripped trailer
4.4.expected_verdict: non-compliant (no trailer means no reverted_sha; diff-verify skipped)

4.5.title: GH 'Revert' button — clean, squash-merged (UI-only)
4.5.notes: Cannot be scripted — requires clicking the Revert button on github.com. See README.

4.6.title: GH 'Revert' button with conflict resolution (UI-only)
4.6.expected_verdict: non-compliant (provenance alone no longer waives; R2 was dropped)
4.6.notes: Cannot be scripted — requires GH UI conflict resolution flow. See README.

4.7.title: GH 'Revert' button, merge-commit strategy (UI-only)
4.7.expected_verdict: non-compliant (revert lives in second-parent chain; audited merge commit isn't a revert)
4.7.notes: Cannot be scripted — UI selection of merge-commit strategy. See README.

4.8.title: Revert-of-revert via the GH UI (UI-only)
4.8.expected_verdict: non-compliant (RevertOfRevert is never classified clean; re-application needs review)
4.8.notes: Cannot be scripted — requires clicking Revert on a revert PR. See README.

4.9.title: Revert-of-revert via local git (legacy message format)

4.10.title: Direct-push revert (no PR)
4.10.expected_verdict: compliant (R1) — clean-revert waiver applies even without a PR
4.10.notes: Revert was pushed directly to main; base came through a PR so the reverted_sha resolves.

4.11.title: Octopus merge (3-way merge)
4.11.expected_verdict: non-compliant (octopus merges are not auto-classified as clean)
4.11.notes: Not a revert scenario per se — included to exercise the octopus path.

4.12.title: Empty revert of empty base
4.12.expected_verdict: compliant (R1 fires trivially; empty-commit fallback would also apply)
